# Skill Library Format Guide

> **Status:** Frozen ground truth for the skills-sync pipeline. Updated only via PR review. Agents load this file as an input — never as something to edit.

This guide is the authoritative spec for how skills in `velt-js/agent-skills` are structured. Updater agents must follow it exactly; never drift from the existing style.

## 1. Editable vs generated files

| File | Editable by pipeline? | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `skills/<s>/SKILL.md` | Cautiously | Only the `description` and prose body. Never reorder frontmatter fields. |
| `skills/<s>/metadata.json` | Cautiously | Bump `version` only on material change. Update `references` only when docs URLs change. Never auto-rewrite `abstract`. See §7 for the two metadata shapes in the wild. |
| `skills/<s>/README.md` | Rarely | Edit only if a contributor-facing instruction changed. |
| `skills/<s>/rules/shared/_sections.md` | Yes (additions) | New categories may be appended. Existing impact levels must not be auto-downgraded. (Legacy fallback: `rules/_sections.md` — preserved if present.) |
| `skills/<s>/rules/shared/_template.md` | Never | Hand-curated scaffold. |
| `skills/<s>/rules/<group>/<cat>/<rule>.md` | **Primary target** | Where most docs-driven changes land. `<group>` is one of `shared/`, `react/`, `non-react/`. Most rules live under `shared/`; only `velt-setup-best-practices` uses `react/` and `non-react/` extensively. |
| `skills/<s>/AGENTS.md` | Never (generated) | Regenerated by `npm run build`. |
| `skills/<s>/AGENTS.full.md` | Never (generated) | Regenerated by `npm run build`. |
| `skills/<s>/evals/**` | Never (eval data) | Out of scope. |
| `*-workspace/**` | Never | Out of scope. Filter at discovery time by checking for `SKILL.md`. |

## 2. Required frontmatter

**`SKILL.md`:**
```yaml
---
name: <kebab-case slug — must equal directory name>
description: <single line, 275–505 chars; lists features + triggering vocabulary; may include "Triggers on any task involving X — even if the user doesn't explicitly say 'Y'." pattern; may cross-reference sibling skills>
license: MIT
metadata:
  author: velt
  version: "<semver>"
---
```

**Rule file (`rules/<group>/<cat>/<rule>.md` where `<group>` is `shared`, `react`, or `non-react`):**
```yaml
---
title: <action-oriented sentence case>
impact: <CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM-HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW-MEDIUM|LOW>
impactDescription: <one short sentence, quantified if possible>
tags: <comma-separated keywords, lowercase, no quotes>
---
```

## 3. Required section structure in `SKILL.md`

```
# <Skill Title>
<one-paragraph opening>

## When to Apply
<bullet list of triggers>

## Rule Categories by Priority
<markdown table: Priority | Category | Impact | Prefix>

## Quick Reference
### <category 1>
- '<prefix>-<rule-slug>' — <one-line summary>
### <category 2>
...

## How to Use
<fenced bash block showing how to read rules>

## Compiled Documents
<reference to AGENTS.md and AGENTS.full.md>
```

## 4. Required section structure in rule files

```
## <Title — same as frontmatter title>
<1–2 sentence rationale>

**Incorrect:**
```<lang>
<bad example with inline comment explaining why>
```

**Correct:**
```<lang>
<good example>
```

<optional framework-specific subsections: **For Next.js (App Router):**, **For HTML/Vanilla JS:**>

<optional **API Key Setup:** or similar numbered steps>

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] <hand-tailored check 1>
- [ ] <hand-tailored check 2>

**Source Pointers:**
- https://docs.velt.dev/<path> — "<section name>"
```

## 5. Hard rules an updater agent must obey

1. **Preserve frontmatter field order** in `SKILL.md`. Adding new fields is forbidden.
2. **Never shorten the `description` field below 250 chars** without an explicit reason logged in the PR body.
3. **Never rewrite `metadata.json.abstract`** (canonical shape) or `metadata.json.author` (compact shape).
4. **Never reorder `references`** in `metadata.json`.
5. **Never touch** files matching `AGENTS.md`, `AGENTS.full.md`, `*-workspace/**`, `**/evals/**`.
6. **Preserve language tags** on every code fence (`jsx`, `typescript`, `html`, `bash`, `python`, `nginx`, `json`, `text`).
7. **Preserve heading levels.** H1 is the title only; section headings are H2; subsections H3.
8. **Add new rules in the appropriate category folder.** If unsure, refuse and emit a `decline.json`.
9. **Bump `metadata.json.version`** by patch (1.0.0 → 1.0.1) when adding or editing rules; by minor (1.0.0 → 1.1.0) when adding a new rule category; by major (1.0.0 → 2.0.0) when the SDK surface itself versioned up. Do not bump on doc-prose-only changes.
10. **Run `npm run validate && npm run build`** before declaring done (the workflow does this via Agent 3, but be aware your edits will be validated).

## 6. Soft rules (style)

- Match imperative voice in rule titles ("Initialize VeltProvider with API Key", not "Initializing").
- Keep paired bad/good code examples. If the existing rule has them, the edit must preserve them.
- Cite source URLs in `Source Pointers:` with section name in quotes.
- Verification checklists should be specific to the rule, not generic.

## 7. Metadata variance

Two `metadata.json` shapes exist in the wild. The build script tolerates both:

- **Canonical (most skills, e.g., `velt-comments-best-practices`):**
  ```json
  { "version": "...", "organization": "Velt", "date": "January 2026",
    "abstract": "...", "references": [...] }
  ```
- **Compact (currently only `velt-proxy-server-best-practices`):**
  ```json
  { "name": "...", "version": "...", "author": "velt",
    "ruleCount": 14, "categories": ["core", "sdk-config", ...] }
  ```

When editing a skill with the compact shape: bump `version`, update `ruleCount` if rules were added/removed, append a new category string to `categories` if a new category was created. **Do not** convert one shape to the other.

## 8. Validator behavior (verified)

`npm run validate` (in `agent-skills/`):
- Exits 1 **only on errors**. Errors: invalid `impact` level, missing `title`, missing `explanation`, parser failures.
- Warnings (not failures): missing `impactDescription`, explanation shorter than 20 chars, missing bad/good example pair, no code in examples.
- **No auto-fix flag.** Agents must edit files themselves to clear errors.

`npm run build` regenerates `AGENTS.md` (compressed) and `AGENTS.full.md` (verbose) for every skill. These changes are expected and must be committed.

`IMPACT_LEVELS` enum: `CRITICAL`, `HIGH`, `MEDIUM-HIGH`, `MEDIUM`, `LOW-MEDIUM`, `LOW`.
